{"id": 781384, "created_at": "2017-05-23T19:54:09Z", "subject": "Japanese on iOS is problematic with \"button shapes\" enabled", "description": "Japanese support is problematic when \"button shapes\" (under accessibility settings) are enabled, especially with kanji. The problem is that the kanji are underlined which makes them look like completely different kanji. For example, \u5341 will look like \u571f which is a completely different character and meaning. Other kanji will look invalid like the underlined version of \u534a. \n\nThis is something that must be fixed as it is confusing and misrepresents the characters.\n\n\n\n", "category_labels": []}
